models.model_label: Extended ROME ontology for Central Bank Digital Currencies (CBDC)

evidence_quotes.quote: In this paper we address this issue by extending and refining our previous work, the Reference Ontology of Money and Virtual Currencies, to provide a semantic foundation for the concept of CBDC.

In the light of the above, we advocate for the need of a reference conceptual model that provides an ontological account to the concept of CBDC, to serve as a basis for communication, consensus and alignment among different approaches and perspectives, as well as to foster interoperability across different applications in the financial industry.

We believe this work clarifies the notion of CBDC, which can serve as a basis for future business ontologies, meaning negotiation, the enactment of law and regulation and the definition of proper governance models.

goals.justification: The paper explicitly frames the model's purpose as clarifying the concept of CBDC and providing a semantic foundation/ontological account, matching the 'clarify', 'define', 'conceptualize', and 'meaning negotiation' signals for For Understanding.

evidence_quotes.quote: In the light of the above, we advocate for the need of a reference conceptual model that provides an ontological account to the concept of CBDC, to serve as a basis for communication, consensus and alignment among different approaches and perspectives, as well as to foster interoperability across different applications in the financial industry.

goals.justification: The authors explicitly state the model is intended to serve as a basis for 'communication, consensus and alignment among different approaches and perspectives', indicating bridging between distinct groups/approaches — matching the 'communicate', 'align stakeholders', and 'bridge between' signals.

evidence_quotes.quote: As future work, we plan to further validate our ontology and use it to support interoperability between payment systems.
as well as to foster interoperability across different applications in the financial industry.

uncertain_goals.reason: Interoperability use is explicitly framed as future work ('we plan to') or as a general aspirational benefit, not as an actual or intended present implementation use of the model — matching the 'future work' negative signal for For Intervening.

evidence_quotes.quote: We believe this work clarifies the notion of CBDC, which can serve as a basis for future business ontologies, meaning negotiation, the enactment of law and regulation and the definition of proper governance models.

uncertain_goals.reason: The phrase 'serve as a basis for future business ontologies' hints at reference use but is too vague and forward-looking to clearly qualify as documentation/knowledge base purpose for the model itself.
